comment: Customer status / parent-child overrides. Used when an Allmoxy customer's status from the payment-recency heuristic is wrong because of a business arrangement that isn't visible in their own Stripe activity. Arrangement types: (a) sub_instance_of_parent — secondary instance billed under a parent's subscription, no standalone MRR but active; (b) comp — instance is live with $0 MRR by sales/owner decision; (c) duplicate_of — same business as another Allmoxy record, usually a renamed or pre-rebrand record; (d) test_artifact — internal sandbox / test record that should be excluded from all customer counts; (e) affiliate_not_customer — Allmoxy affiliate or consultant, not a direct customer (their revenue is Connect/affiliate-side, not subscription); (f) false_positive_needs_review — high-evidence indication the record represents an active customer but parent record isn't established yet, FLAGGED for owner sign-off before final classification; (g) playbook_churned — customer completed HubSpot's Cancellation Playbook AND has $0 current MRR. Force status='churned' so they stop appearing in the active cohort. Per-case owner sign-off per the project_pay_status_cancelled_semantic memo. Applied by _etl_scripts/apply_customer_status_overrides.mjs after amortization; surfaced on the Adjustments Register so a QoE reviewer can audit every status override in one place.

overrides.reason: Tree Products (treeproductsawi.allmoxy.com) is a secondary Allmoxy instance billed under the AWI parent (#323) $199/mo subscription, not a standalone payer. Their own Stripe activity stopped Jan 2024 when the billing consolidated under the parent; instance remains live as part of the parent agreement. Counting them as churned would mis-state retention; counting their MRR as $100/mo would double-count. Resolution: status=active, MRR=$0 (parent already carries the dollars).
overrides.evidence: HubSpot company 6088910932 (Architectural Woods LP) is shared by both #323 and #225; user-confirmed (2026-06-12) the Tree Products instance is active under the parent's subscription.

overrides.reason: Ruck Cabinet Doors (#315) is a duplicate / pre-rebrand record for the same business that operates today as Thomas Creek (#318). The Ruck record has $0 lifetime, 0 transactions, no HubSpot ID — a phantom shell. Thomas Creek (#318) carries the full $134K lifetime and $2,610/mo active MRR. Without this override, Ruck appears as a $0 churn (false signal); with it, both records resolve cleanly to the same active customer.
overrides.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ruck Cabinet Doors is the same business as Thomas Creek. Thomas Creek (#318) Stripe transactions span 99 charges over multi-year history; Ruck (#315) has 0 transactions and no HubSpot company link — consistent with a renamed Allmoxy account.

overrides.reason: Ultra Craftsmanship (#428) is the same business as Ultrashelf (#322) — a pre-rebrand record. Both have payment history (#428 paid through Jun 2023 with $31K lifetime; #322 took over and paid through Oct 2023 with $3.25K lifetime). Without dedup, this single business inflates the churned-logo count by 1. With this override, Ultra Craftsmanship mirrors Ultrashelf's churned status, MRR is zeroed (no double-count of historical churn dollars), and excluded_from_logo_count keeps it out of the logo waterfall as a separate entity.
overrides.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) Ultra Craftsmanship is the same business as Ultrashelf. Timing pattern (Ultra Craftsmanship Stripe activity ends Jun 2023; Ultrashelf activity continues through Oct 2023) is consistent with a mid-2023 rebrand and Allmoxy account rename. #428 has no HubSpot link; #322 maps to HubSpot company 3414592775.

overrides.reason: Massachusetts Design Corp. (#468) rebranded to Julian Woods in 2025. HubSpot has them on a single Company record now labeled 'Julian Woods(formerly Massachusetts Design)' (HubSpot company ID 46167759542, originally 8474228273 — remapped on company merge). Same primary contact (Will Gascoigne); active CSM relationship — 64-minute meeting Dec 19 2025, $750 deal closed Apr 2026. Our Allmoxy customer record #468 still carries the old 'Massachusetts Design Corp.' name and shows as churned because the rebrand didn't update our records. No separate Julian Woods record exists in customer_profiles yet — when one is created, link it as the canonical via parent_allmoxy_customer_id. Until then, marking #468 as duplicate_of (orphaned canonical) so it falls out of churn counts.
overrides.evidence: HubSpot company 46167759542 (Julian Woods, formerly Massachusetts Design) — notes from 2025-12-19 (production order customization meeting), 2025-09-11 (Will Gascoigne meeting), 2025-09-02 (William Gascoigne meeting). Customer is actively engaging Allmoxy CSM team into late 2025 / early 2026.

overrides.parent_customer_name: Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated
overrides.reason: Wildwood Cabinets, Inc. (#308) is the same business as Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated (#457) — a pre-rebrand record. #308 paid through Feb 2022 (lifetime $7K, 21 txns) before the rebrand; #457 took over with the new DBA name and is currently active (lifetime $21K, 122 txns, $419/mo MRR). Without dedup, this single business would show up as both a 2022 churn AND an active customer. With this override, #308 mirrors #457's active status, MRR is zeroed, and excluded_from_logo_count keeps it out of the logo waterfall.
overrides.evidence: User-confirmed (2026-06-12) — HubSpot custom-object record 2-39181518/22267106658 links Modern Fronts and Wildwood Cabinets. #308 HubSpot company 2551073029 (Wildwood Cabinets, Inc.); #457 HubSpot company 37315206430 (Modern Fronts DBA - Wildwood Cabinets Incorporated). Account naming + payment-timeline transition consistent with a 2022 rebrand.
